title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: How $800 Monthly Car Payments Are Hurting Car Sales
items.description: Cars have become so expensive that many Americans are putting off or not buying new cars, hurting the auto industry.

items.title: Politicians Are Trying to Control the News
items.description: The rising global attacks on the free press reveal increasingly desperate regimes attempting to snuff out attempts to find the truth.

items.title: FEMA Delays Under Kristi Noem Angered Republicans
items.description: Long delays in FEMA assistance brought bipartisan complaints, especially in North Carolina after Hurricane Helene in 2024.

items.title: Nature Report, Killed by Trump, Is Released Independently
items.description: A draft assessment of the health of nature in the United States is grim but shot through with bright spots and possibility.

items.title: Silicon Valley legend Vinod Khosla has ‘no plans to leave California’ amid billionaire tax uproar—but he has another idea to fix the wealth loophole
items.description: Imagine a world where taxes just "go away" for everyone making less than $100,000 a year, Khosla told Fortune.

items.title: Korean startup Wrtn is on track to pass $100M in annual recurring revenue, riding a loneliness-epidemic-fueled boom in AI entertainment
items.description: Chief product officer Dong-jae Lee uses the analogy of a dungeon master for a tabletop RPG: The AI constructs a narrative in response to user choices.

items.title: SpaceX is poised to raise more money in its IPO than was raised in last year’s 90 IPOs combined
items.description: And that means Wall Street will make more money, too.

items.title: After Apalachee High School Shooting, a Hunger for Accountability
items.description: The country is casting an ever wider net in its search for accountability after mass shootings. Charging parents is an emerging tactic.

items.title: Israel Pushes Farther Into Lebanon, and Readies for More
items.description: Days into its offensive against Hezbollah, Israel is massing armored vehicles near the Lebanese border for a potentially much larger ground incursion.

items.title: Why Torpedoed Iranian Warship Is a Problem for India
items.description: An Iranian ship had been invited by India to take part in peacetime naval exercises with dozens of other countries, days before it was sunk in a U.S. ...

items.title: A.I. Videos Distort Damage From Iranian Strikes
items.description: After Iran’s retaliatory strikes across the Middle East, A.I.-generated imagery claiming to show massive explosions or exaggerated destruction at or n...

items.title: Live Updates: Trump Says He Should Have Role in Picking Iran’s Leader
items.description: Israel threatened more strikes in Lebanon as the Middle East crisis spilled beyond Iran. Italy pledged air defense weapons to help Gulf nations defend...

items.title: Trump Says He Should Have Role in Picking Iran’s Leader
items.description: The president’s comments were the most explicit he has been yet about his vision of an American role in creating a new government in Tehran.

items.title: The Fidelity CEO’s surprise record as a crypto visionary
items.description: Fidelity’s Abigail Johnson has had the vision and courage to pursue a strategy even when her Big Finance peers shied away.

items.title: Polychain raises $200 million for fourth crypto VC fund, shakes up staff
items.description: The firm founded by Coinbase alum Olaf Carlson-Wee is shifting its focus.
